rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF SARA R. JULIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Approval Of Conservator'S And Attorneys' Fees And Costs Advanced; And For Approval Of Periodic Payments
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. In July 2017, the Court settled the former conservator Gene Marchi's first account covering a period from 10/29/2015 through 10/31/2016 and approved fees of the former conservator's attorneys in the total amount of $11,873.18 for their legal services rendered from 12/29/2015 through 04/28/2017. In April 2019, the Court approved fees of the former conservator's attorneys in the total amount of $57,102.73 for their legal services rendered from 04/29/2017 through 02/21/2019. The current conservator's attorneys, who also represented the former conservator, now request fees of $8,928 for 29.7 hours of their legal services rendered from 10/26/2015 through 12/28/2015; and from 09/23/2016 through 02/23/2017. The attorneys explain that the fees "were mistaken not requested" as part of the former conservator's first accounting and subsequent fee petitions filed and adjudicated in 2017 and 2019 respectively. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF ALLISON WING CHUNG LUI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/16/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Still no proposed order(s). The attorney declaration filed on 3/17/22 does not fully address the qualifications of the "paralegal." Business and Professions Code Section 6450(d): Every two years, commencing January 1, 2007, any person that is working as a paralegal shall be required to certify completion of four hours of mandatory continuing legal education in legal ethics and four hours of mandatory continuing legal education in either general law or in an area of specialized law. All continuing legal education courses shall meet the requirements of Section 6070. Certification of these continuing education requirements shall be made with the paralegal's supervising attorney. The paralegal shall be responsible for keeping a record of the paralegal's certifications. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/6/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ALEX L. RODRIGUEZ AND JULIA G. RODRIGUEZ TRUST dated July 30, 2001, as restated and amended
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Order
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: Armondo Shepard has not been given proper notice. The petition listed his mailing address, and he was provided notice thereon, but Petitioner acknowledged in a subsequent declaration that he had not lived there since 2003/2004, and no one knows his whereabouts. Declarations regarding search and his whereabouts have been filed. No specific allegation has been made to show that Jose Hernandez was recently asked about Armondo Sherhard's whereabouts. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JONATHAN NGUYEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/16/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies. No proposed order. No proof of service. No form DE-147S. If second generation or more distant heirs are listed in item 8, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] In a verified supplement to the petition, the petitioner must set forth names and dates of death of the parents and whoever his parent is who is/was the decedent's sibling. Waivers of bond have not been filed. Have the persons having priority declined to act? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/6/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
